description: This file controls the overlay graphics related to states, auras, cast animations, curses, and buffs. Any column field name starting with "*" is considered a comment field and is not used by the game.
source: Colonnes du .txt (BKVince 3.2) ; types/descriptions du guide TXT eezstreet-d2rdoc.

columns.name: overlay
columns.description: Defines the name of the overlay, used in other data files

columns.name: Filename
columns.description: Defines which DCC file to use for the Overlay

columns.name: version
columns.description: Defines which game version to use this Overlay (0 = Classic mode | 100 = Expansion mode)

columns.name: Character
columns.description: Used for name categorizing Overlays for unit translation mapping

columns.name: PreDraw
columns.description: Boolean field. If equals 1, then display the Overlay in front of sprites. If equals 0, then display the Overlay behind sprites.

columns.name: 1ofN
columns.description: Controls how to randomly display Overlays. This value will randomly add to the current index of the Overlay to possibly use another Overlay that is indexed after this current Overlay. The formula is as follows: Index = Index + RANDOM(0, [.1ofN]-1).

columns.name: Xoffset
columns.description: Sets the horizontal offset of the overlay on the unit. Positive values move it toward the left and negative values move it towards the right.

columns.name: Yoffset
columns.description: Sets the vertical offset of the overlay on the unit. Positive values move it down and negative values move it up.

columns.name: Height1
columns.description: These are additional values added to .Yoffset. Only 1 of these .Height# fields are added, and which field that gets selected depends on the monstats2.OverlayHeight field value (Example: If the monstats2.OverlayHeight value is 4, then use the .Height4 field). If the monstats2.OverlayHeight value is 0, then ignore these .Height# fields and add a default value of 75 to .Yoffset. Player unit types will always use .Height2.

columns.name: Height2
columns.description: These are additional values added to .Yoffset. Only 1 of these .Height# fields are added, and which field that gets selected depends on the monstats2.OverlayHeight field value (Example: If the monstats2.OverlayHeight value is 4, then use the .Height4 field). If the monstats2.OverlayHeight value is 0, then ignore these .Height# fields and add a default value of 75 to .Yoffset. Player unit types will always use .Height2.

columns.name: Height3
columns.description: These are additional values added to .Yoffset. Only 1 of these .Height# fields are added, and which field that gets selected depends on the monstats2.OverlayHeight field value (Example: If the monstats2.OverlayHeight value is 4, then use the .Height4 field). If the monstats2.OverlayHeight value is 0, then ignore these .Height# fields and add a default value of 75 to .Yoffset. Player unit types will always use .Height2.

columns.name: Height4
columns.description: These are additional values added to .Yoffset. Only 1 of these .Height# fields are added, and which field that gets selected depends on the monstats2.OverlayHeight field value (Example: If the monstats2.OverlayHeight value is 4, then use the .Height4 field). If the monstats2.OverlayHeight value is 0, then ignore these .Height# fields and add a default value of 75 to .Yoffset. Player unit types will always use .Height2.

columns.name: AnimRate
columns.description: Controls the animation frame rate of the Overlay. The value is the number of frames that will update per second.

columns.name: LoopWaitTime
columns.description: Controls the number of periodic frames to wait until redrawing the Overlay. This only works with Overlays that are a loop type.

columns.name: Trans
columns.description: Controls the alpha mode for how the Overlay is displayed, which can affect transparency and blending

columns.name: InitRadius
columns.description: Controls the starting Light Radius value for the Overlay (Max = 18)

columns.name: Radius
columns.description: Controls the maximum Light Radius value for the Overlay. This can only be greater than or equal to .InitRadius. If greater than .InitRadius, then the Light Radius will increase in size per frame, starting from .InitRadius until it matches the .Radius value (Max = 18)

columns.name: Red
columns.description: Controls the Red color gradient of the Light Radius

columns.name: Green
columns.description: Controls the Green color gradient of the Light Radius

columns.name: Blue
columns.description: Controls the Blue color gradient of the Light Radius

columns.name: NumDirections
columns.description: The number of directions in the cell file

columns.name: LocalBlood
columns.description: Controls how to display green blood or VFX on a unit.

columns.name: WeaponStateFlags
columns.description: Controls a bit field flag where each value specifies which state the overlay can react to (used for vfx).

columns.name: WeaponStateGroup
columns.description: Specify a filter to have the overlay react only for states that belong to the specified group.

columns.name: StartSound
columns.description: Optional sound to play when the overlay is started.
